seoTitle: Set Existing Contacts as Marketing Contacts in HubSpot
metaDescription: Learn how to reclassify existing HubSpot contacts as marketing contacts using bulk edit, and how to keep an eye on your monthly subscription limits.
intro: HubSpot bills separately for non-marketing records and marketing-eligible contacts, so if you want to email an existing group you'll need to flip their status manually. This guide walks through filtering your contacts, selecting the right records, and running the bulk update to mark them as marketing contacts. It also covers watching your account limits so you don't trigger an unexpected billing tier.

steps.heading: Open your Contacts directory
steps.body: Go to the primary Contacts index in HubSpot to see your full database in table form.

steps.heading: Filter to the right segment
steps.body: Use search filters or saved views to isolate the specific existing records you want to update.

steps.heading: Select the target rows
steps.body: Check the master selection box in the table header to highlight all the records you plan to change at once.

steps.heading: Run the bulk action
steps.body: Click the More dropdown in the table menu bar and choose Set as marketing contacts from the options.

steps.heading: Confirm the change
steps.body: Type the required confirmation keyword and click the update button to flip the eligibility status for the selected batch.

steps.heading: Check your billing limits
steps.body: Review your account and billing settings to monitor total marketing contact usage and avoid surprise charges.

faq.question: Does setting a contact as marketing-eligible cost extra?
faq.answer: Yes, marketing contacts count against your subscription tier, so it's worth checking your usage before doing a large bulk update.

faq.question: Can I undo marking contacts as marketing contacts?
faq.answer: You can typically switch them back to non-marketing status the same way, through the bulk action menu in Contacts.

faq.question: Why do I need to type a confirmation keyword?
faq.answer: It's a safety step to prevent accidentally reclassifying a large batch of contacts and affecting your billing.

faq.question: Where do I check how many marketing contacts I'm using?
faq.answer: Your Account & Billing settings show a usage meter for total live marketing contacts against your plan's limit.
